JOHN de Burgh ([1290]-Galway 18 Jun 1313).  m (Waltham Abbey, Essex 30 Sep 1308) as her first husban …

zh.lifeSummary: 當John de Burgh於1286年出生在愛爾蘭武爾斯特時，他的父親Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster27歲，母親Margaret Guines27歲。他於1308年9月30日與Elizabeth de Clare在英國艾可沃爾坦 艾比結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1313年1月18日在愛爾蘭貝爾法斯特享年27歲。

zh-hans.Waltham Abbey, Essex, England: 英国艾可沃爾坦 艾比
zh-hans.Carrick Castle, County Kildare, Ireland: 爱尔兰庫迪 吉爾達卡里克 卡素
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John de Burgh于1286年出生在爱尔兰武爾斯特时，他的父亲Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster27岁，母亲Margaret Guines27岁。他于1308年9月30日与Elizabeth de Clare在英国艾可沃爾坦 艾比结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1313年1月18日在爱尔兰貝爾法斯特享年27岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

en.lifeSummary: When John de Burgh was born in 1286, in Ulster, Ireland, his father, Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster, was 27 and his mother, Margaret Guines, was 27. He married Elizabeth de Clare on 30 September 1308, in Waltham Abbey, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died on 18 January 1313, in Belfast, Ireland, at the age of 27.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John de Burgh est né en 1286, à Ulster, Irlande, son père, Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Margaret Guines, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth de Clare le 30 septembre 1308, à Waltham Abbey, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 18 janvier 1313, à Belfast, Irlande, à l’âge de 27 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als John de Burgh 1286 in Ulster, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster 27 und seine Mutter Margaret Guines 27. Er hat am 30. September 1308 in Waltham Abbey, Essex, England Elizabeth de Clare geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 18. Januar 1313 im Alter von 27 Jahren in Belfast, Irland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John de Burgh è nato il nel 1286 nel Ulster, Irlanda, il suo padre, Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Guines, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth de Clare il 30 settembre 1308, nel Waltham Abbey, Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 18 gennaio 1313, nel Belfast, Irlanda, all'età di 27 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: John de Burghが1286年にアイルランドウルスターで生まれた時，父Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulsterは27歳，母Margaret Guinesは27歳であった。1308年9月30日にイギリスエセックスワルサム アビーでElizabeth de Clareと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1313年1月18日にアイルランドベルファストで27歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John de Burgh(이)가 1286년 아일랜드 울스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Guines(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1308년 9월 30일 영국 에세스 월섬 애브브이에서 Elizabeth de Clare(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1313년 1월 18일 아일랜드 벨파스트에서 27세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John de Burgh nasceu em 1286, em Ulster, Irlanda, seu pai, Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Guines, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth de Clare em 30 de setembro de 1308, em Waltham Abbey, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 18 de janeiro de 1313, em Belfast, Irlanda, com 27 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John de Burgh родился в 1286 г. в местности улстир, Ирландия, его отцу по имени Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Guines, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth de Clare 30 сентября 1308 г. в местности валтам эбби, эссекс, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер 18 января 1313 г. в местности белфаст, Ирландия в возрасте 27 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John de Burgh nació en 1286, en Ulster, Irlanda, su papá, Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Margaret Guines, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Elizabeth de Clare el 30 de septiembre de 1308, en Waltham Abbey, Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió el 18 de enero de 1313, en Belfast, Irlanda, a la edad de 27 años.
